articles.title: Stories behind Ozempic shaadis: ‘How did this mota guy get a girl like you?’
articles.description: A growing number of Indians are turning to GLP-1 injections like Ozempic for rapid weight loss, primarily driven by wedding pressures and social media ideals. While these drugs are effective for obesity, medical professionals express concern over their cosmetic use, potential side effects, and the risk of counterfeit products. Experts warn against short-term use for quick results, emphasizing the need for medical supervision and a holistic approach to weight management.

articles.title: Watch: Neymar breaks down in tears after Brazil's heartbreaking World Cup exit
articles.description: Brazilian football legend Neymar has played his final international match after a tearful farewell following Brazil's World Cup exit. The 34-year-old, who debuted at the same New Jersey stadium where his career concluded, battled injury throughout the tournament. His departure marks the end of a decorated 16-year journey, leaving Brazil to usher in a new era.

articles.title: Ram Temple Trust to hold key meeting amid donation row today: What's on agenda
articles.description: The Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust convenes a critical meeting in Ayodhya amidst an embezzlement probe. Discussions will center on the resignations of key members Champat Rai and Anil Mishra, potential new administrative structures, and a briefing on the ongoing investigations into donation misuse. The trust will also review financial statements and consider appointing a CEO to oversee temple management.

articles.title: Was Delta flight struck by July 4 fireworks in Chicago? Pilot says 'felt a big bang'
articles.description: A Delta flight landing in Chicago on July 4th reported a startling 'big bang' as it was apparently struck by fireworks at a low altitude. Air traffic control acknowledged multiple similar incidents, even warning another pilot of a 'war zone.' While the Delta flight landed safely with no injuries and no damage found, the FAA has launched an investigation into the dangerous close calls during the holiday celebrations.

articles.title: Russia launches fresh missile, drone attack on Kyiv ahead of Nato summit in Turkey
articles.description: Kyiv Mayor Vitaliy Klitschko said debris from intercepted missiles and drones caused fires and damage in at least two districts of the capital. Air raid sirens had sounded across the city shortly before the explosions. The latest bombardment came a day after Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky warned that Russia was preparing another large-scale attack.

articles.title: Jewellery likely to be worth €4m stolen from French museum, probe on
articles.description: Millions of euros worth of exquisite jewellery vanished from the Lalique museum in Wingen-sur-Moder, France, after a daring pre-dawn burglary. Thieves targeted the jewellery room, making off with approximately twenty pieces. The estimated loss is substantial, potentially reaching four million euros. The museum, a tribute to Rene Lalique's artistry, will remain closed for several days following the incident.

articles.title: Putin & Zelenskyy hold separate calls with Trump
articles.description: A Ukrainian strike on Russian-occupied Crimea claimed one life and injured two others early Sunday. This comes as both Ukrainian and Russian presidents held separate calls with US President Donald Trump, discussing potential peace initiatives. Kyiv aims to isolate the peninsula, while Moscow insists on diplomatic solutions respecting its core interests. Trump's envoys are reportedly involved in mediation efforts.

articles.title: Stock market today: BSE Sensex jumps 300 points, NSE Nifty50 opens above 24,350
articles.full_text: Asian equity markets started the week on a positive note as easing oil prices and hopes of a steady US interest rate outlook lifted investor sentiment. Wall Street futures also traded higher ahead of the upcoming earnings season in the United States. South Korea's benchmark index continued its strong run, supported by optimism around artificial intelligence-related chip demand, while Nikkei traded marginally lower. Around 8:40 am IST, Japan's Nikkei was trading 1.18% lower, while South Korea's Kospi tumbled 3%. In contrast, Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index (HSI) bucked the trend, rising 0.78%. Investors globally are increasingly shifting their attention from monetary policy concerns towards corporate earnings, particularly expectations of strong profits from technology companies benefiting from the AI boom. Positive global cues could provide additional support to the Dalal Street at the opening bell. Stock market today: Top stocks to buy this week Among India's most valuable listed companies, Bharti Airtel and Bajaj Finance emerged as the biggest wealth creators during the previous week. Bharti Airtel added over Rs 36,500 crore to its market capitalisation, taking its valuation above Rs 11.63 lakh crore. Bajaj Finance followed closely with an increase of more than Rs 33,000 crore. ICICI Bank, LIC, HDFC Bank and Hindustan Unilever also posted gains in market value. The surge reflects continued investor confidence in sectors ranging from telecom and financial services to consumer goods, even as broader market sentiment remained supported by improving domestic fundamentals and favourable global developments. Apart from domestic earnings, investors across global markets are awaiting fresh signals from the US Federal Reserve. Following softer-than-expected US labour market data, expectations have grown that the central bank may avoid another interest rate hike in the near term. Futures currently indicate a strong possibility that the Fed will leave rates unchanged at its July meeting. Attention now turns to the minutes of the Fed's June policy meeting, scheduled later this week, which could offer insights into policymakers' assessment of inflation and economic growth. Any indication of a less aggressive monetary stance would likely support equities worldwide, including markets such as India. Oil prices are likely to remain one of the biggest drivers for Indian equities this week as global crude continues to trade at relatively comfortable levels. Brent crude has slipped near four-month lows while US crude also eased after OPEC+ agreed to further raise production targets from August. Concerns over supply disruptions through the Strait of Hormuz have also moderated, easing pressure on global energy markets. For India, which imports the bulk of its crude oil requirements, softer prices could help contain inflation, improve the current account balance and reduce import costs. Stable energy prices are therefore expected to provide an important cushion for domestic markets. The spotlight this week is shifting to corporate earnings as Tata Consultancy Services prepares to announce its June-quarter results on July 9, marking the start of the Q1 FY27 earnings season. Investors will closely examine the company's financial performance along with management commentary on demand conditions, discretionary technology spending and the pace of artificial intelligence-led business opportunities. Market experts believe earnings guidance from India's largest IT services exporter could set the tone for the broader technology sector and influence overall market sentiment. Apart from headline numbers, investors will also monitor management's outlook for margins, client spending patterns and revenue visibility in the coming quarters. Dalal Street is entering the new trading week with momentum firmly on their side after benchmark indices ended last week with healthy gains. The BSE Sensex advanced 663.44 points, or 0.86%, while the NSE Nifty climbed 214.85 points, or 0.89%. Investors drew confidence from resilient domestic macroeconomic indicators, robust GST collections and signs of improving industrial activity, which reinforced expectations of sustained economic growth. Positive global cues also helped sentiment after softer-than-expected US labour market data strengthened hopes that the Federal Reserve could maintain a more accommodative monetary policy. Market participants will now watch whether these supportive factors can help Indian equities extend their recent rally.

articles.title: Bank holiday today: Where are banks shut on July 6? Check state-wise list
articles.description: Plan your bank visits carefully as several branches will be closed on specific dates this July due to regional holidays. West Bengal and Mizoram observe closures today, July 6. Further state-specific holidays are scheduled across Meghalaya, Odisha, Uttarakhand, Manipur, Sikkim, and Tripura. Remember, Sundays and the second and fourth Saturdays also mean branch closures. Digital banking services remain unaffected.

articles.title: Precious metals rate today: Gold holds near 2-week high; silver extends gains
articles.full_text: Gold recorded a weekly gain of more than 2% last week, ending a four-week losing streak after weaker-than-expected US payroll data improved sentiment towards the precious metal. The rebound came as investors reduced expectations of prolonged high interest rates following signs of a cooling US labour market, allowing bullion to recover from recent lows. Gold held near a two-week high on Monday, but gains remained capped as the US dollar strengthened. Spot gold traded around $4,174.66 per ounce, with analysts saying easing expectations of Federal Reserve rate hikes have supported bullion, while a firmer dollar has limited further upside. A stronger greenback makes gold more expensive for buyers using other currencies, weighing on international demand. Physical gold demand in India softened after prices rebounded from a three-month low, while buying interest in China improved slightly. Meanwhile, JPMorgan lowered its outlook for gold demand this year, saying purchases from key sectors may be weaker than previously expected. The bank now forecasts gold prices to average $4,300 per ounce in the third quarter and $4,500 per ounce in the fourth quarter, suggesting further gains could be gradual despite recent market recovery. Silver continued its upward momentum on Monday, extending gains for a fifth consecutive session. Spot silver edged higher to $62.47 per ounce after earlier touching its strongest level since June 23. The white metal has outperformed gold in recent sessions, supported by improving investor sentiment and stronger industrial demand expectations. Platinum and palladium also remained in positive territory, continuing their recent recovery in global bullion markets. The release of the minutes from the Federal Reserve's June 16-17 policy meeting is expected to be the key event for bullion markets this week. Investors will closely examine discussions from the first meeting chaired by Kevin Warsh for signals on inflation and future interest rate decisions. Analysts say the outcome could influence the direction of gold prices, alongside upcoming US economic data including services PMI, trade figures and weekly jobless claims. A slowdown in US job growth has reduced expectations of a near-term interest rate hike by the Federal Reserve, lending support to gold prices. June payroll growth came in weaker than expected, while employment figures for the previous two months were revised lower. According to the CME FedWatch Tool, traders now see around a 55% chance of a September rate hike, down from over 60% before the jobs report. Lower interest rates generally improve gold's appeal as it is a non-yielding asset. Gold prices traded near a two-week high on Monday after softer-than-expected US jobs data eased concerns over an immediate Federal Reserve interest rate hike. Spot gold held around $4,175 per ounce, while US gold futures for August delivery climbed to $4,186.80. The precious metal posted a gain of more than 2% last week, snapping a four-week losing streak as investors shifted back towards safe-haven assets amid expectations of a less aggressive US monetary policy.

articles.title: Mumbai University postpones July 6 exams as IMD alert, monsoon chaos bring city to a halt
articles.description: The University of Mumbai has postponed all undergraduate and postgraduate examinations scheduled for July 6, 2026, following persistent rainfall and an IMD weather advisory. Citing severe waterlogging and transport disruptions across the city, the university prioritised student safety. A revised examination timetable will be announced soon, while existing hall tickets will remain valid for the rescheduled exams.

articles.title: AIIMS New Delhi declares results for more than 460 faculty posts using blockchain technology
articles.description: The All India Institute of Medical Sciences (AIIMS), New Delhi, has announced the selection results for more than 460 faculty posts across over 50 disciplines at AIIMS New Delhi and the AIIMS-Central Armed Police Forces Institute of Medical Sciences (CAPFIMS) campus. Conducted over six months, the recruitment process received more than 3,200 applications and used blockchain digital technology, digitally locked marks and automated merit list generation to ensure confidentiality, transparency and quicker declaration of results.

articles.title: Mumbai schools shut today as BMC declares holiday amid heavy rain alert
articles.description: Schools and colleges across Mumbai, Thane and Palghar remained closed today after authorities declared aholiday following the India Meteorological Department's orange alert for heavy rainfall. The precautionary measure was taken to ensure student safety as heavy showers and gusty winds are forecast. While government and private offices continue to function, citizens have been advised to avoid unnecessary travel. Online classes may be conducted if required, with heavy rain continuing to disrupt normal life.

articles.title: JoSAA Counselling 2026 round 3 seat allotment result today; check reporting schedule and admission process
articles.description: The JoSAA Counselling 2026 round 3 seat allotment result will be announced at 5 pm on July 6, 2026, on the official website. Candidates allotted seats must complete online reporting, document upload and fee payment by the prescribed deadlines. They can also choose the Float, Freeze or Slide option based on their admission preference. JoSAA is conducting six counselling rounds for admissions to IITs and other participating institutes through the centralised seat allocation process.

articles.title: Taylor Swift did the bouquet toss at her wedding
articles.description: In a breathtaking fusion of classic and personal, Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s wedding dazzled stars and fans alike. The unforgettable event included a unique bouquet toss, with Ashley Smith from the Kansas City Chiefs delightingly catching Swift's flowers. Held at Madison Square Garden, the private ceremony featured heartfelt handwritten vows, a melodic performance by Swift, and Adam Sandler presiding, making the guest list of about 1000 truly memorable.
